title: Zapoteca amazonica
intro: Zapoteca amazonica, also known as "Amazonian Zapote," is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Zapoteca amazonica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
